_comment: CROSS-LANGUAGE CANONICALIZATION FIXTURE (issue #751).
These values are produced by the NODE's Rust operator-action code
(botho/src/operator_action.rs) for the deterministic signing key
SigningKey::from_bytes(&[1u8; 32]). The browser composer MUST reproduce the
EXACT canonical byte string, the EXACT Ed25519 signature over
'botho-operator-action-v1' || canonical_bytes, the EXACT signerKeyId
(blake2b-256(pubkey)[..8] hex), and the EXACT envelopeHash
(blake2b-256(canonical_bytes) hex). If any of these drift, the node's
parse-after-verify signature check fails — the #1 failure mode for this
feature. action-envelope.test.ts asserts byte-equality against every entry.
DRIFT GUARD (issue #759): this fixture is a hand-maintained snapshot, so a
change to the node's canonicalization could silently pass the JS test above
against a stale snapshot while real signatures break at the node. The Rust
test `fixture_matches_node_canonicalization_no_drift` in operator_action.rs
reconstructs every entry below from the seed [1;32] via build_signed/sign_str
and asserts the canonical string, signature, and envelopeHash match this file
byte-for-byte. It runs under `cargo test --workspace`, so node-side drift
fails CI here instead of in production.
To regenerate (only when a canonicalization change is INTENTIONAL, e.g. a new
v2 envelope field): update the build_signed/sign_str test helpers and/or the
KNOWN_KEYS allowlist in operator_action.rs, then temporarily print the
canonical string, signature_hex, and blake2b_256_hex(canonical) for each
entry from a Rust test (seed [1;32]) and paste them here. Update this file and
the drift-guard test's expectations together, then re-run `cargo test
--workspace` and `pnpm test:run` in web/ to confirm both sides agree.
